sections.paragraph: an unused root, which seems to have had the signification of making a noise, raging, similarly to the cognate words, רָעַם, רָהַב; also הָמָה, הָמַם. Hence רָהָם, Arab. رُهَامُ multitude, in the pr. name אַבְרָהָם. From that lost form it appears to me that, by softening the letters, have sprung both לְאֹם and Arab. دَهْمُ multitude.
